DECLARATION (SUPPLEMENTAL) OF DAVID BALTER IN SUPPORT OF REPLY MEMO FILED BY PLAINTIFF STATE LABOR COMMISSIONER DIVISION OF LABOR STANDARDS ENFORCEMENT, DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS, STATE OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER STATE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY RESPONDENT HAS FAILED TO ATTEND ITS DEPOSITION AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ORDERED RESPONDENT DHL TO PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO PETITIONER'S SUBPOENAS ON OR BEFORE 04/26/06. A DECLARATION OR AFFIDAVIT UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY BY PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE WHY DHL CONTENDS THERE ARE NO DOCUMENTS TO PRODUCE TO THOSE DOCUMENTS SOUGHT BY PETITIONER. DEPOSITION OF PERSON(S) WHO SIGNED DECLARATION TO TAKE PLACE ON 05/24/06. ALL DATES SET BY THE COURT MAY BE MODIFIED BY COUNSEL. PETITIONER TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN, REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause Why Respondent Has Failed To Attend Its Deposition And Produce Documents
Off Calendar Apr-12-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff/Petitioner State Labor Commissioner's Order To Show Cause Why Respondent Has Failed To Attend Its Deposition And Produce Documents.
APPEARANCE: David Balter, Staff Counsel, Department of Industrial Relations, Division of Labor Standards Enforcement 703-4863 for petitioner & moving party; Timothy J. Murphy, Esq. of Fisher & Phillips LLP 510/287-3611 for deft/respondent & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court ordered respondent DHL to produce all documents responsive to petitioner's subpoenas on or before 04/26/06. In addition, a declaration or affidavit under penalty of perjury by person(s) most knowledgeable why DHL contends there are no documents to produce to those documents sought by petitioner. Deposition of person(s) who signed declaration to take place on 05/24/06. If more than one person, depositions to take place on same day or immediately thereafter. All dates set by the Court may be modified by counsel. Petitioner to prepare proposed form of order. (301/JLW/DM) Judge: James L. Warren, Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
